The search successfully retrieved the exact paper requested: "WILDS: A Benchmark of in-the-Wild Distribution Shifts" by Koh et al. (ICML 2020). This paper is highly relevant as it directly addresses the gap between standard i.i.d. benchmarks and real-world deployment scenarios by providing a unified collection of datasets with natural, in-the-wild distribution shifts. It will be cited in the introduction and related work to summarize existing literature on distribution shifts, introduce key real-world datasets, highlight the limitations of artificial corruptions, and support arguments for adopting robust, domain-aware evaluation methodologies. It does not overlap with the existing citations.
